title: Static fields should be avoided in generic types
description: Static fields should not be used in generic typesAnalyze your codeintentionality-completemaintainabilityCode SmellWhy is this an issue?A static field in a generic type is not shared among instances of different closed constructed types, thusLengthLimitedSingletonCollection<int>.instancesandLengthLimitedSingletonCollection<string>.instanceswill point to different objects, even thoughinstancesis seemingly shared among allLengthLimitedSingletonCollection<>generic classes.If you need to have a static field shared among instances with different generic arguments, define a non-generic base class to store your static members, then set your generic type to inherit from the base class.Noncompliant code examplepublic class LengthLimitedSingletonCollection<T> where T : new() { protected const int Max
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Static field in generic type - each closed generic type will have its own copy of this field

never_trigger_on: Classes inheriting from a base class (already defined static there)
static readonly fields with simple values
logic.reasoning: Static fields in generic types are not shared across different type arguments. LengthLimitedSingletonCollection<int>.instances and LengthLimitedSingletonCollection<string>.instances point to different objects. This is often unexpected behavior.

should_trigger: public class Generic<T> { protected static int instances; }
public class Cache<T> { private static Dictionary<string, T> cache; }
should_not_trigger:
public class SingletonBase { protected static int instances; }
public class Generic<T> : SingletonBase { }
public class Generic<T> { protected const int MaxSize = 10; }
